pr.title: Migrate `.text` section of `ftcoll` to C and match a few functions
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Migrated the fighter collision `ftcoll` text object from `asm/melee/ft/ftcoll.s` into `src/melee/ft/ftcoll.c`, deleting the old 6867-line assembly file and building `ftcoll.c.o` instead. A small leading set of functions remained/moved as C, including combo/knockback-related routines such as `func_800763C0`, `func_80076444`, `func_8007646C`, `func_800764DC`, `func_80076528`, `func_800765AC`, `func_800765E0`, and `func_800765F0`; the rest of the migrated text was preserved as inline asm blocks in the C file. Data labels were split into `asm/melee/ft/ftcoll_data.s`, declarations were moved from the global `include/functions.h` toward `src/melee/ft/ftcoll.h`, and consumers gained explicit `#include <melee/ft/ftcoll.h>` includes.

key_files.role: Main migration target; received the former `ftcoll` text body, with early matched C functions and many inline asm fallback blocks.
Deleted source assembly text file, removing 6867 lines.
New data-label assembly file for `ftcoll` constants/data.
Swapped build input from `asm/melee/ft/ftcoll.s.o` to `src/melee/ft/ftcoll.c.o` plus `asm/melee/ft/ftcoll_data.s.o`.
Expanded local `ftcoll` declarations, including functions such as `func_8007ABD0`, `func_8007B868`, `func_getWindOffsetVec_8007B924`, and `func_8007BE3C`.
Removed now-localized `ftcoll` declarations from the global function-declaration header.
Several fighter/character files added explicit `#include <melee/ft/ftcoll.h>` after declarations moved out of `functions.h`.

what_changed: Deleted `asm/melee/ft/ftcoll.s` and added the corresponding code to `src/melee/ft/ftcoll.c`.
Changed `obj_files.mk` so the text build uses `$(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/ft/ftcoll.c.o` and adds `$(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/ft/ftcoll_data.s.o`.
Added `asm/melee/ft/ftcoll_data.s` with global float/data labels such as `lbl_804D8314`, `lbl_804D8318`, and `lbl_804D831C` shown in the diff excerpt.
Kept many not-yet-decompiled routines as inline asm in `ftcoll.c` using `#pragma push`, `asm unk_t func_...()`, `nofralloc`, `clang-format off`, and `#pragma pop`.
Moved or exposed `ftcoll` prototypes in `src/melee/ft/ftcoll.h` and removed a block of declarations from `include/functions.h`.
Added `#include <melee/ft/ftcoll.h>` to multiple dependent fighter files, including Donkey, GameWatch, Koopa, Luigi, Mars, Ness, Pikachu, Yoshi, and `fighter.c` per changed-file evidence.
Cleaned decomp.me links from matching C functions in `ftcoll.c`; review discussion says links are useful for `NON_MATCHING` functions but should be removed from matching ones.
smart_moves: Used an incremental migration: move the whole text section into a C compilation unit while preserving unmatched routines as inline asm, allowing the asm file to be deleted without requiring every function to be decompiled immediately.
Split code and data at the build level by adding `ftcoll_data.s.o` alongside `ftcoll.c.o`, avoiding loss of label-controlled constants/data during text migration.
Localized function declarations into `src/melee/ft/ftcoll.h` and updated consumers to include that header, reducing reliance on the broad `include/functions.h` bucket.
Maintained exact assembly for hard functions with `nofralloc` inline asm blocks, preserving matchability while enabling nearby C functions and header cleanup.
Removed decomp scratch links from matching functions, following the review-confirmed convention that decomp.me links are for `NON_MATCHING` work-in-progress functions.
decomp_lessons: Large `.s` files can be migrated safely by compiling a C file that mixes matched C at the top with inline asm for the remaining routines; this creates a future decomp surface without forcing a full rewrite.
When replacing an asm text object with a C object, audit data labels separately and keep them in a dedicated data `.s` object if needed.
Moving declarations out of `include/functions.h` requires adding explicit module headers to all dependent source files; this PR demonstrates the pattern with `#include <melee/ft/ftcoll.h>`.
For matched functions, remove stale decomp.me scratch links to reduce noise; keep links only where they document active `NON_MATCHING` attempts.
Preserve address-based temporary names such as `func_8007B868` and `lbl_804D82E0` when semantics are not yet known; do not rename beyond available evidence.
naming_conventions: Unknown functions retained address names like `func_800763C0`, `func_800765F0`, `func_8007B868`, and `func_8007BE3C`.
Unknown data/string/float labels retained `lbl_` address-style names such as `lbl_804D82E0`, `lbl_804D82E4`, `lbl_803C0C58`, and `lbl_804D3A68`.
Function parameters and comments used common fighter naming such as `fighter_gobj`, `attacker`, `victim`, `attackID`, `fp`, `Fighter*`, `HSD_GObj*`, `Hitbox*`, and `ftHurt*`.
One semi-semantic function name appeared in the header: `func_getWindOffsetVec_8007B924`, combining a descriptive name with the original address suffix.
Header include convention used module-local includes like `#include <melee/ft/ftcoll.h>` rather than relying on global declarations.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted standalone assembly but preserved unmatched code as inline asm in `src/melee/ft/ftcoll.c`.
Inline asm blocks used `#pragma push`/`#pragma pop`, `asm unk_t func_...()`, `nofralloc`, and `// clang-format off/on` to protect exact instruction layout.

Static float constants and extern labels were declared near inline asm blocks, e.g. `static f32 const lbl_804D82E0 = 0.0F;`, `static f32 const lbl_804D82E4 = 500.0F;`, and extern string/assert labels.
The migrated file retained raw instruction comments and labels such as `lbl_80076698`, making future C decompilation easier to map against the original assembly.
The build-object swap in `obj_files.mk` is the core matching tactic: replace the asm text object with a C object while adding a separate data object to maintain layout.

review_feedback.author: stephenjayakar
review_feedback.feedback: Requested decomp URLs be pasted in, giving `https://decomp.me/scratch/mL8R8` as an example and noting more were missing.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Clarified that omitted decomp links were intentional: decomp links are useful for `NON_MATCHING` functions, but should be removed from matching ones.

follow_up_queries: Which inline asm routines in `src/melee/ft/ftcoll.c` remain after PR 576 and are good candidates for conversion to matching C?
Can `lbl_80459278`, `lbl_804D6558`, and related `ftcoll` collision buffers be typed into named structs?
Do all files that call `ftcoll` routines include `src/melee/ft/ftcoll.h` directly rather than relying on `include/functions.h`?
Can `func_getWindOffsetVec_8007B924` guide naming of adjacent wind/collision helper functions?
Was `tools/convert_data.py` later restored or generalized after being retargeted to `**/ftcoll_data.s`?
